acceptance_criteria: Approving a Work Graph writes an attributed, content-hashed approval record distinct from candidate approval
A Run may only capture a Work Item from an approved Work Graph whose content hash still matches the approval
summary: Record human approval of a Work Graph as evidence, distinct from candidate approval

acceptance_criteria: Merge runs only when approval is current for the exact merge candidate and repository policy permits it
The Merge Agent records merge evidence and never grants approval itself
summary: Constrained Merge Agent that merges only a current Approved Revision after deterministic gates

acceptance_criteria: Required checks pass for the exact merge candidate in a clean environment
Protected-branch policy is verified before the merge proceeds
summary: Clean-environment CI gate and protected-branch policy checks before merge

acceptance_criteria: Authoritative checks run against the merged commit and record evidence
A failure stops further shipping and produces a human-reviewed Recovery Proposal
summary: Post-Merge Verification against the resulting target-branch commit, with human-reviewed Recovery Proposals on failure

acceptance_criteria: A deployment adapter ships an approved, post-merge-verified revision behind the adapter boundary
Deployment changes no workflow state semantics or approval authority
summary: Deployment adapters after merge safety is demonstrated

acceptance_criteria: Proposals are generated from recurring Run Evidence and evaluated against fixtures and historical failures
A proposal never changes a baseline until it passes evaluation
summary: Generate Improvement Proposals from repeated Run Evidence and evaluate them against fixed fixtures

acceptance_criteria: Baseline changes require explicit verification and human approval through an Adoption Gate
Upstream skill changes can be compared and selectively adopted through the same gate
summary: Require an Adoption Gate before changing skills, Repository Profiles, role prompts, or workflow defaults
